The term "pseudo-diptère" (pseudo-dipteral) indicates that the temple appears to have a double row of columns (as in a "diptère" or dipteral temple), but in reality, it only has a single row of columns with a wider space between the columns and the cella (the inner chamber of the temple).

In essence, a "Périptère pseudo-diptère" temple combines elements of both peripteral and dipteral designs, giving the illusion of a more complex structure while maintaining a simpler, single row of columns.
